item.dc:creator: Littlemisselle

item.title: 10 years?
item.description:
That's crazy. It's been ten years. So much has happened. What a crazy decade.
We had a catnip party.
Chessie is digging it
Puddles is...uh... Gonna take some savings and get Chinese food for the holiday tomorrow! Maybe. Maybe we just eat Mac and cheese. Lol. I don't know if imgur is gonna last another decade, honestly, but if not, I'd like to be actual pen pals with some of you. Let me know. I need a hobby that requires some sort of socialization.
item.dc:creator: TinyOctopus

item.dc:creator: hoopsnek

item.title: Yankee Pot Roast for a classic Nor’easter
item.description:
When a nor’easter parks itself off the Jersey coast, there’s no better time to stay in and let something slow-cook in the oven. This pot roast fits the mood precisely. It’s steady, familiar, and built for weather like this. The wind can do what it wants outside. Inside, the kitchen smells like rosemary, red wine, and garlic. It’s the kind of meal that makes the house feel sturdy again, the kind you can ladle out while the storm keeps moving through.
A little fresh parsley from the garden brings much needed color and a fresh note to balance all of the earthy flavors going on in this dish.
I don’t know how to make this slow motion, but I think it’s darn sexy.
Yankee Pot Roast Ingredients: Rosemary Salt: 1 tablespoon kosher salt 1 teaspoon freshly cracked black pepper 1 tablespoon fresh rosemary, finely chopped Meat Prep: 4-pound beef chuck roast 2 tablespoons neutral oil (canola or vegetable) Deglazing & Sauce Base: 1 large onion, diced 8 garlic cloves, lightly smashed 2 tablespoons tomato paste 3 tablespoons flour ﻿﻿2 tablespoons oil (if needed, after flour is added) ﻿﻿1 cup red wine ﻿﻿1 tablespoon Worcestershire sauce ﻿﻿1 tablespoon balsamic vinegar ﻿﻿1 teaspoon soy sauce ﻿﻿½ teaspoon anchovy paste Broth Additions (Added in 3 Stages): 4 cups beef broth or stock, added in 3 parts Vegetables & Herbs (Added Later): 4 large carrots, cut into chunks 1/2 pound baby potatoes (or large potatoes cut into chunks) 2 stalks celery 8 sprigs fresh thyme 2 sprigs rosemary Instructions: 1. Prep & Sear the Meat 1. ﻿﻿﻿Preheat oven to 325°F (163°C). 2. ﻿﻿﻿Mix salt, pepper, and chopped rosemary to create the rosemary salt. 3. ﻿﻿﻿Pat the chuck roast dry with paper towels and season generously on all sides with the rosemary salt mixture. 4. ﻿﻿﻿Heat 2 tablespoons oil in a stainless steel pan over medium-high heat. 5. ﻿﻿﻿Sear the beef for 3-4 minutes per side until deeply browned. Remove and set aside. 6. Reduce heat to medium and add the diced onion. Sauté for 2-3 minutes, scraping up browned bits. 7. Add garlic cloves (I screwed up and minced it, but used it anyway) , cook for 1 more minute. Transfer mixture to enameled pot with lid, for the rest of the recipe. Put pot on medium heat. 8. ﻿﻿﻿Stir in 2 tablespoons tomato paste, cooking for 5 minutes until slightly darkened. 9. ﻿﻿﻿Reduce heat to medium-low, then add 3 tablespoons flour. Stir constantly for 1-2 minutes. 10. ﻿﻿﻿﻿Add 2 tablespoons oil (if needed) and cook for another 3 minutes, making sure the flour doesn't burn. 11. ﻿Pour in 1 cup red wine, scraping up any remaining bits from the bottom. Simmer for 5 minutes to reduce. 12. ﻿﻿﻿﻿Stir in 1 tablespoon Worcestershire sauce, 1 tablespoon balsamic vinegar, soy sauce, and anchovy paste. 13. ﻿﻿﻿﻿Slowly add 4 cups of beef broth in 3 additions, a little over 1 cup at a time, bringing it to a simmer and letting it cook for 5 minutes between each addition. 14. ﻿﻿﻿﻿Return the seared chuck roast to the pot, ensuring it's mostly submerged. 15. ﻿﻿﻿﻿Cover with a cracked lid (slightly ajar) to allow for some evaporation. 16. ﻿﻿﻿﻿Place in a 325°F oven for 2 hours. 17. ﻿﻿﻿﻿Remove the pot from the oven, add the carrots, potatoes, celery, rosemary, and thyme sprigs, ensuring they are partially submerged in the broth. 18. ﻿﻿﻿﻿Cover again (lid still cracked) and return to the oven for another 90 minutes. 19. Remove from the oven and let the roast rest for 45 minutes in the covered pot. 20. ﻿﻿﻿﻿Skim any excess fat from the surface and remove the thyme sprigs. 21. ﻿﻿﻿﻿Serve sliced or shredded with the vegetables and rich broth, and some nice warm rolls or fresh bread.
item.dc:creator: girlintraffic

Go away…di*k

item.dc:creator: CptCheesePants

item.title: So proud of my city and fellow car enthusiasts who made a 3 year old boy's dream come true
item.description:
This is Tucker Langford When his Kansas City family asked for a parade to celebrate their 3-year-old son Tucker, the community showed up in a big way. On Saturday, the Kansas City area came together to create an unforgettable parade for three-year-old Tucker as he was discharged from Children’s Mercy Hospital for home hospice care. Tucker is courageously fighting Dystrophic Epidermolysis Bullosa (EB), or “butterfly skin.” In his honor, his family organized this event, celebrating his love for trucks, Jeeps, motorcycles, hot rods, and emergency apparatus. A fire department who'd been contacted by the family to do a possible drive by put out the word Friday morning and it became bigger than anyone imagined. I fueled up my 1968 Dodge Coronet R/T 440 up and made the 40 minute trek up to join what turned out to be over 1,000 classic cars, trucks, Jeeps, as well as emergency vehicles, utility trucks, even horseback riders and a couple of camel riders. It took around 2 hours to make my way through to Tucker's viewing location with his family. He smiled and waved at everyone beaming like a light bulb. I was amazed and happy at the outpouring of support for this young man and his family to make his final days happy as humanly possible. All in 24 hours notice. Several of the old classics suffered some minor mechanical issues due to the long idling times, but fellow car enthusiasts and spectators helped pitch in to help them out. While I didn't get to see them, several Chiefs players showed up in their vehicles to support. Edited to include a small GoFundMe set up to help Tucker and his family https://gofund.me/14c762bdd
Tucker having the time of his life
Staging area was an old mall being refurbished
Camels amongst the classic Chevy club
New friends bring made while waiting
item.dc:creator: PrinceMaxx7

item.description:

20

23
24
25
26 Imgur Creator Spotlight: @OtterCaper did this watercolor as part of a storybook. Check out their posts to see more.
27 Man Tax: Robbie Amell
“Anyone can forget about talent, but if you're also a good person, your name will be remembered for a long time.” - Alex Rodriguez I try to keep my posts "good vibe zones". So, instead of finding the one thing in here you don't like and commenting only on that, also comment about something you DID like. Balance the negativity. Love you long time. So, I made a discord server. It's a relaxed environment where you can take/donate memes to the cause, play trivia twice a week, chat about lots of topics or about nothing at all. We're also setting up Jack Box game night and movie night! Very laid back/relaxed vibe. Come hang with us. https://discord.gg/AWFV7FT3RQ I'm also on the imgcat server for updates & fun: https://discord.gg/TpjD37jgJ9 https://ko-fi.com/mememuseumcurator Unable to work and pretty destitute. Feel free to leave me a tip. Those that have, you are the best. You've helped me so much.
item.dc:creator: MemeMuseumCurator

item.title: Why would Trump pick an idiot like Pete Hegseth as the Secretary of Defense?
item.description:
Let’s not concentrate on the stylistic misogyny of using the words “men” and “females” in the same sentence. There are mums in Ukraine who are successfully running Air Defense, shielding millions of civilians in cities from brutal attacks that include hundreds of missiles and drones, shooting down 90–97% of targets. Some decorated by Zelenskyy himself. Many of them probably wouldn’t pass Hegseth’s ‘standards.’ But Hegseth’s army would be shredded to pieces by 19-year-old Ukrainian girls who drop bombs from drones with the skill of a Play Station controller.
A lot of medals of valor in that room — and not the kind you get for fake bone spurs. Obviously, Trump picked Hegseth because he wanted someone like him in the position of the “Secretary of War.” Which tells you everything you need to know about both of them.
A failed TV host and a 5-time army dodger came to lecture decorated U.S. generals who went through several wars, on “warrior ethos.” Trump and Hegseth’s show clearly embarrassed the U.S. military leadership.
item.dc:creator: LunaAtlas

item.title: Managers are not the victim here
item.description: The Gen Z employees at my company make memes mocking the company values and other usual corporate propaganda. And because they don’t buy into the corporate loyalty bullshit and treat work as a strictly transactional relationship, they are labeled as “difficult to work with”. We also keep hiring these guys at below market rate and then scratch our heads when we can’t retain them. But you know, I’m sure we can find the right lanyard and water bottle combo to give out during new employee orientation that will help with retention. /s
item.dc:creator: DownvotesStarWars

item.title: This Week's Quotes - GovBrief Quotes 1
item.description: Playing with some website extraction for people who don't get the newsletter.
item.dc:creator: GeorgeBounacos

item.title: GovBrief Today #255
item.description: ⚠️ Violence is Trump’s default response. Federal agents fired pepper balls at naked cyclists in Portland while priests were illegally denied access to prisoners in Chicago. In DC, JD Vance threatened more painful mass firings while his boss flew to the Middle East and threatened to give Ukraine long-range missiles. Remember January 6. Mike Pence, protected by his Secret Service detail, still hid with them in a Capitol parking garage. He understood that Trump throws the levers of power, especially violence, against everyone who stands up to him. Even his hand-picked VP wasn't safe. Neither are we. That's exactly why showing up in overwhelming numbers matters. Bullies back down when they see the crowd against them. This Saturday, October 18, join millions at No Kings protests nationwide. Find your local event at nokings.org. And don't leave truth to the algorithm. Our free and private nightly email arms you with images, data, and quotes so you can see it all for yourself. Add your email at govbrief dot today or on Substack at Gov Brief Today. #GovBriefToday #Resist Subscribe (It's Free!) - https://gbounacos.substack.com [1] https://go.govbrief.today/trump-jan6-claim-fbi [2] https://go.govbrief.today/trump-heaven-chances-october [3] https://go.govbrief.today/trump-ukraine-tomahawk-missiles [4] https://go.govbrief.today/religious-leaders-ice-chicago [5] https://go.govbrief.today/smithsonian-closes-shutdown [6] https://go.govbrief.today/trump-scavino-personnel-promotion [7] https://go.govbrief.today/gov-shutdown-vance-threats [8] https://go.govbrief.today/naked-cyclist-protest-ice
item.dc:creator: GeorgeBounacos

item.title: Seattle getting a national guard invasion in 3,2,1
item.description:
Probably a minute before the Trump administration will retaliate, but we can enjoy it for the second it lasts. Tried to tag political, but it's not available [get it together Imgur]!
Sauce: SEA Airport won't play video of Kristi Noem blaming Democrats for government shutdown | king5.com https://www.king5.com/article/news/local/sea-airport-wont-play-video-kristi-noem-blaming-democrats-government-shutdown/281-30734f89-6445-4f17-9df6-033f90660edb
